extraction_reasoning: The litigation table separates matters filed by and against each entity: I counted the Against our Company row (criminal 1, tax 23, statutory/regulatory 0), the Against our Subsidiary row (all 0), and the Against our Promoters row for the criminal count (1); I excluded every By row and excluded promoter matters from the tax and aggregate-amount fields. The quantified aggregate amount against the company and subsidiary is the stated ₹522.06 million for the company plus nil for the subsidiary, converting ₹522.06 million ÷ 10 to ₹52.206 crore; the promoter amount of ₹98.14 million is excluded. The contingent-liabilities table has no stated Total row, so I summed the contingent-liability items as of December 31, 2024—₹1,587.69 million, ₹144.72 million and ₹0.37 million—while excluding the ₹0.87 million capital commitment; ₹1,732.78 million ÷ 10 equals ₹173.278 crore. The company-wide customer concentration for the latest full fiscal year, Fiscal 2024, is 26.96% for the top five and 43.08% for the top ten, both stated as percentages of revenue from operations. The litigation and contingent-liability tables use ₹ million, converted to crore at 1 crore = ₹10 million; no going-concern or statutory-auditor opinion status is mentioned in the provided risk section.
